title: Ocugen Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Ocugen Q3 2021 Earnings Call Summary. Major Business Developments: Covaxin Progress: The company submitted an emergency use authorization request to the FDA for Covaxin in the 2-18 age group, citing an unmet need particularly for children ages 2-5. CEO Shankar Musunuri stated the data make a compelling efficacy and safety case to the agency. They also filed an IND application for a Phase III immuno-bridging study comparing US and Indian populations, with plans to file a biologics license application in the second half of 2022. Gene Therapy Advancement: Ocugen filed an IND for OCU400, their lead modifier gene therapy candidate for Retinitis Pigmentosa. The company expanded its manufacturing partnership with CanSino Bio to support both OCU400 and OCU410 (designed for dry AMD). Financial Overview: For Q3 2021, R&D expenses: $6.3 million (vs. $1.5M year-over-year). General/administrative expenses: $4.5 million (vs. $1.7M). Net loss: $10.8 million. Cash position: $107.5 million. Regulatory Outlook: Management suggested a potential FDA approval timeline for adults in early 2023 if fast-track designation is granted.
